purpose: Current production bundle debt ceiling. The v3.51.25 export completeness and artifact-integrity boundary measures 4,587,149 raw / 1,316,233 gzip JavaScript bytes on the reviewed Windows source tree. Against the v3.51.24 clean source artifact (4,578,450 / 1,313,642), the delta is +8,699 raw / +2,591 gzip (under 0.20%) for the shared exact-count pager, stable 500-row paging, 100,000-row fail-closed ceiling, provider row schemas, identity/count-drift checks, legacy enrichment batching and interval sweep, and exact SpreadsheetML worksheet limits across direct generators and export flows. The largest chunk remains 1,785,150 raw / 567,949 gzip. No database, Edge wire contract, runtime dependency, CSS size or route was added. The reviewed ceilings retain only 128 raw / 279 gzip bytes of total cross-platform and release-SHA tolerance; the existing largest-chunk ceilings are not increased. Further increases require an explicit measured baseline review.
